title: The Sect Master's Secret
content:
After the celebration, Chen Fan spent the next few days enjoying his time with Lin Xue. They walked through the sect grounds, talked about their hopes and dreams, and shared their deepest secrets.

But Chen Fan couldn't forget about the traitor. He knew that the Demonic Sect was still out there, and he needed to find out who was working with them.

One day, while Chen Fan was meditating, Old Man Ye visited him.

"You're distracted," Old Man Ye said.

"I'm thinking about the traitor," Chen Fan said.

Old Man Ye nodded. "Good. You should be. But you also need to focus on your relationship with Lin Xue."

Chen Fan sighed. "I know. But I can't help but worry about the danger."

"Danger is everywhere," Old Man Ye said. "But love is worth the risk."

He stood up. "Come with me. I have something to show you."

Old Man Ye led Chen Fan to the hidden chamber behind the forbidden area. Inside, there was a stone tablet covered in ancient runes.

"This is the Tablet of Secrets," Old Man Ye said. "It contains the secrets of the Heavenly Sword Sect, including the identity of the traitor."

Chen Fan's eyes widened. "Really?"

Old Man Ye nodded. "But you need to prove yourself worthy before you can see the secrets."

"How?"

"You need to pass the Trial of the Sword," Old Man Ye said. "It's a test that only the strongest disciples can pass."

Chen Fan nodded. "I'll do it."

Old Man Ye smiled. "Good. The trial is tomorrow."

***

The next day, Chen Fan went to the Trial of the Sword. It was held in a large chamber deep beneath the sect grounds.

Inside the chamber was a series of obstacles - traps, puzzles, and illusions. Chen Fan had to navigate through them all to reach the end.

The first obstacle was a room filled with swinging blades. Chen Fan used the Heavenly Sword Art to cut through the blades, clearing a path.

The second obstacle was a puzzle involving ancient runes. Chen Fan used his knowledge of the Heavenly Sword Sect's history to solve the puzzle.

The third obstacle was an illusion - Chen Fan saw himself standing in the forbidden chamber, dying at the hands of Zhang Wei and Lin Xue.

"No!" Chen Fan shouted, shaking off the illusion.

He continued forward, reaching the final obstacle - a sword floating in the air.

"Take the sword," a voice said.

Chen Fan reached out and took the sword. As soon as he touched it, he felt a surge of power flowing into his body.

He had passed the Trial of the Sword.

***

After the trial, Old Man Ye showed Chen Fan the Tablet of Secrets.

"The traitor is..." Old Man Ye said, his voice trailing off.

"Who?"

Old Man Ye looked at Chen Fan. "It's Zhang Wei."

Chen Fan's heart sank. "Zhang Wei? My friend?"

Old Man Ye nodded. "He's been working with the Demonic Sect for years. He's the one who betrayed you in your past life."

Chen Fan's mind raced. Could this be true? Zhang Wei, the boy he had grown up with, the friend who had always been by his side...

"Why?"

"Because he's jealous of you," Old Man Ye said. "He's always been in your shadow, and he wants to be the one in the spotlight."

Chen Fan closed his eyes, trying to process this information.

"What should I do?"

"You need to confront him," Old Man Ye said. "But be careful. He's dangerous."

Chen Fan nodded. "I will."

***

That night, Chen Fan went to find Zhang Wei.

"Zhang Wei," Chen Fan said, walking into his room.

Zhang Wei looked up, surprised. "Chen Fan? What are you doing here?"

"I need to talk to you," Chen Fan said.

Zhang Wei nodded, motioning for him to sit.

"What is it?"

"I know," Chen Fan said.

"Know what?"

"I know you're working with the Demonic Sect," Chen Fan said.

Zhang Wei's face changed. He stood up, his eyes wide.

"How did you know?"

"Old Man Ye told me," Chen Fan said.

Zhang Wei sighed, sitting back down.

"It's true," he said. "I've been working with the Demonic Sect for years."

"Why?"

"Because I'm tired of being in your shadow!" Zhang Wei said, standing up again. "You're always the hero, always the one who gets all the praise. What about me? What about my efforts?"

He walked to Chen Fan, his eyes filled with anger.

"I trained just as hard as you," he said. "But you always outshone me. The Sect Master favored you, Lin Xue loved you, and everyone looked up to you."

"I didn't ask for any of this," Chen Fan said.

"I know!" Zhang Wei said. "But that doesn't make it easier."

He sat back down, his voice softening.

"I'm sorry, Chen Fan. I never wanted to hurt you. But I couldn't help myself."

Chen Fan looked at Zhang Wei, his heart filled with sadness. He had lost his friend, and he didn't know if he could ever get him back.

"What are you going to do?" Zhang Wei asked.

Chen Fan stood up. "I'm going to give you a chance. Leave the Demonic Sect, and I'll forgive you."

Zhang Wei looked at him, surprised. "Really?"

Chen Fan nodded. "Yes. But if you don't, I'll have to report you to the Sect Master."

Zhang Wei sighed. "I'll think about it."

Chen Fan walked away, leaving Zhang Wei sitting alone in his room.

He didn't know if Zhang Wei would take his offer, but he hoped he would.

For the sake of their friendship.
